findings.title: Broker escape achieved with 3 alternative privileged-action paths evaluated and blocked
findings.statement: The 5-stage renderer-to-broker chain is fully validated with GDB breakpoints at each stage boundary. From broker context, multi-route analysis evaluated 3 paths to privileged action: (1) direct kernel ioctl blocked by seccomp filter, (2) broker-mediated IPC to privileged helper blocked by PID-based caller validation, (3) shared-memory corruption of kernel-mapped region blocked by read-only mapping. All three are hard blockers requiring additional primitives not available from the current chain.

evidence.summary: gdb and strace show renderer leak, write primitive, and broker escape with validated stage boundaries
multi-route: 3 privileged-action paths evaluated — seccomp blocks ioctl, PID validation blocks helper IPC, read-only mapping blocks shared-memory corruption
